purpose: Obtain a genuinely external proof review of the bounded post-W5 USD result without allowing package repair, author coaching, or internal implementation diversity to substitute for reviewer independence.

review_target.primary_claim: Within the frozen candidate universe and registered benchmark, FARA and LTS-PROV are successful, non-equivalent, incomparable bounded minima; no universal kernel is established.

required: ability to read formal specifications and proof obligations
familiarity with state-transition systems, formal reasoning, or equivalent technical methods
no authorship or implementation role in Project FAR or the reviewed USD artifacts
no access to private author reasoning or unpublished repair guidance
disclosure of financial, professional, personal, and intellectual conflicts
agreement to evaluate the frozen package before any author response
disqualifying: participation in construction of any reviewed mapping, validator, proof, or synthesis
shared generation of reviewed artifacts under the same agent-controlled workflow
acceptance of substantive author coaching before filing the initial verdict

freeze_and_access.release_unit: one immutable Git commit or signed archive containing the complete manifest
freeze_and_access.freeze_event: reviewer access to any substantive package artifact
freeze_and_access.post_access_rule: no reviewed artifact may be repaired in place after freeze; corrections require a new package version and the original verdict remains preserved

mandatory_review_questions: Are all source scopes frozen independently of the winning vocabularies?
Do the constructions preserve the registered structural, semantic, operational, dependency, information, and historical obligations?
Are derived machinery, adapters, decoders, canonicalizers, and invariants fully charged?
Are equivalence and Pareto comparisons applied consistently without scalarization or hidden tie-breaking?
Do ablation results establish only bounded local necessity rather than global necessity?
Does the minimality result depend on a non-exhaustive candidate universe?
Are invariance transformations genuine commitment-preserving changes rather than semantic alterations?
Are actual-process and external-independence claims correctly withheld?
Is any theorem, fixture, validator, or prose statement stronger than the evidence supports?
Can a concrete counterexample, omitted cost, invalid equivalence, or scope dependency block the primary claim?

required_outputs.reviewer_declaration: identity, expertise, independence, conflicts, access date, and package identifier
required_outputs.finding_register: one record per finding with severity, artifact, exact location, rationale, and claim effect
required_outputs.obligation_matrix: pass, qualified, fail, or unresolved for every mandatory review question

severity_rules.blocking: invalidates the primary bounded claim, changes the minimal frontier, defeats preservation, exposes uncharged required machinery, or shows independence ineligible
severity_rules.major_nonblocking: materially narrows interpretation or requires prominent qualification without invalidating the bounded result
severity_rules.minor: clarity, notation, documentation, or non-load-bearing implementation issue
severity_rules.observation: suggestion not affecting the registered claim

decision_rule.confirmed: all mandatory questions resolved pass and no blocking finding
decision_rule.confirmed_with_nonblocking_qualification: no blocking finding and at least one qualified result requiring preserved caveat
decision_rule.blocking_objection: one or more unresolved blocking findings supported by a reproducible argument or counterexample
decision_rule.unresolved: eligibility, evidence access, or technical ambiguity prevents a justified terminal verdict

nonclaims: an eligible reviewer has been recruited
the package has been externally accessed
the bounded result has been independently confirmed
global universality or actual-process correspondence is established
